target_phenotype: Morphological cell phenotype — cell and nuclear shape, texture, and intensity features across multiple channels

readout.measurement: High-content fluorescence imaging of fixed cells
readout.instrument_or_platform: Optical pooled screening (in situ sequencing + imaging)
readout.primary_metric: Morphological feature vectors — cells are clustered by similarity of phenotypic profiles across imaging channels

clustering.method: Phenotypic similarity clustering of per-gene morphological profiles

controls.positive_controls: Known pathway members used to validate cluster assignments

provenance.citation: Funk et al. (2022) Nat Methods. Optical pooled screens in human cells.
provenance.data_source: funk_2022.csv — gene-level phenotypic cluster assignments from the Funk et al. 2022 dataset

notes: Clusters represent sets of genes with similar morphological phenotypes. Genes in the same cluster likely perturb the same biological process or pathway
